description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence clearly, concisely, and logically, so listeners can follow the line of reasoning; ensure that the organization, development, substance, and style are appropriate to purpose, audience, and task.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence clearly and in an organized way so others can easily follow their thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's, perspective, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, identifying any fallacious reasoning or exaggerated or distorted evidence.
description_plain: Your child can judge how well a speaker explains their ideas and uses evidence, and spot any mistakes or exaggerations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's, perspective, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric, identifying any fallacious reasoning or exaggerated or distorted evidence.
description_plain: Your child can judge how well a speaker explains their ideas and uses evidence, and spot any mistakes or exaggerations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse media or formats evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can bring together information from different sources and check if each one is trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multiple sources of information presented in diverse media or formats evaluating the credibility and accuracy of each source.
description_plain: Your child can bring together information from different sources and check if each one is trustworthy and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views and understanding and make new connections in light of the evidence and reasoning presented.
description_plain: Your child can thoughtfully respond to different opinions, summarize what people agree or disagree on, and explain their own views using evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Respond thoughtfully to diverse perspectives, summarize points of agreement and disagreement, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views and understanding and make new connections in light of the evidence and reasoning presented.
description_plain: Your child can thoughtfully respond to different opinions, summarize what people agree or disagree on, and explain their own views using evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by asking and answering questions, including others, and making sure ideas are clear and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Propel conversations by posing and responding to questions that relate the current discussion to broader themes or larger ideas; actively incorporate others into the discussion; and clarify, verify, or challenge ideas and conclusions.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by asking and answering questions, including others, and making sure ideas are clear and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can work with others to set rules, goals, deadlines, and roles for group discussions or projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Work with peers to set rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, clear goals and deadlines, and individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can work with others to set rules, goals, deadlines, and roles for group discussions or projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared and uses what they have read or researched to support their ideas in the conversation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read and researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence from texts and other research on the topic or issue to stimulate a thoughtful, well-reasoned exchange of ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grades 9–10 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group discussions on grade-level topics, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Initiate and participate effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grades 9–10 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can join in different types of group discussions, listen to others' ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly and convincingly.

description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words for school subjects and everyday life, and can figure out what words mean on their own when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze documents of historical and literary significance, from the Americas, for their themes, purposes, and rhetorical features.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective or purpose in a text in which the rhetoric is particularly effective, analyzing how the rhetoric contributes of the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why an author wrote something and how the author's way of writing helps get their point across.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's perspective or purpose in a text in which the rhetoric is particularly effective, analyzing how the rhetoric contributes of the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze how an author uses and refines the meaning of a key term or terms over the course of a text.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze a complex set of ideas or sequence of events and explain how specific individuals and groups, ideas, or events interact and develop over the course of the text.

subject: Language Arts

Provide an evidence-based summary of the text that includes analysis.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop and connect, and write a summary that uses evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another, and matters left uncertain, in order to provide a complex analysis. description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how they develop and connect, and write a summary that uses evidence from the text.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can explain how the way an author organizes a story, play, or poem affects its meaning and the way it makes readers feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author's choices concerning how to structure specific parts of a text contribute to its overall structure and meaning as well as its aesthetic impact.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the way an author organizes a story, play, or poem affects its meaning and the way it makes readers feel.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including creative or technical uses, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in the text, including figurative, connotative and technical meanings; analyze the impact of word choice on meaning and tone, including words with multiple meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including creative or technical uses, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a literary text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the impact of the author's choices regarding how to develop and relate elements of a literary text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author’s choices affect the way a story’s characters, setting, and events are developed and connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more themes or central ideas of a text and analyze their development over the course of the text, including how they interact and build on one another to produce a complex account. Provide an evidence-based summary of the text that includes analysis.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas or themes in a story and explain how they grow and connect throughout the text, using evidence from the story to support their explanation.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can explain what a text says directly and what it suggests, using strong evidence from the text, and can point out parts where the text is unclear or leaves questions unanswered.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze what the text says explicitly as well as inferentially, including determining where the text leaves matters uncertain; cite strong and thorough textual evidence to support analysis. Identify areas where the text leaves ideas unclear or unexplored.
description_plain: Your child can explain what a text says directly and what it suggests, using strong evidence from the text, and can point out parts where the text is unclear or leaves questions unanswered.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can speak in complete sentences when it fits the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas, thoughts, or feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child can clearly describe people, places, things, and events with helpful details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone else says to better understand or learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in something read aloud or shown in other ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions if they are confused about what is being discussed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can join in conversations by responding to what others say, going back and forth several times.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: With help, your child can follow the agreed-upon rules for group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations about first grade topics and books with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visual aids to help share information and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and reasons in a way that is organized and easy for listeners to follow, using the right style for the audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge what a speaker thinks, how they support their ideas, and how they try to convince others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information given in different ways, such as by listening, looking at pictures, or reading charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in different kinds of conversations, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases they learn from talking, reading, and listening, including words that show simple relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner and adjectives differing in intensity by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that mean almost the same thing by explaining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use.
description_plain: Your child can show how words connect to real-life situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes.
description_plain: Your child can define words by what group they belong to and by describing important features.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups to better understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child can show they understand how words are related and that some words have slightly different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words and their inflectional forms.
description_plain: Your child can spot common root words and their different forms (like 'look' and 'looked').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common word parts (like 'un-' or '-ed') to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the rest of the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words in first grade books and topics by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 2.
description_plain: This skill starts in second grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can try to spell new words by sounding them out and using what they know about spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words and words with regular spelling patterns correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas in dates and to separate words in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use the correct punctuation at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for names and dates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can make and expand different types of sentences (statements, questions, commands, and exclamations) when asked.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions.
description_plain: Your child can use common prepositions (like 'in,' 'on,' and 'under') correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners.
description_plain: Your child can use words that help specify which person or thing they mean (like 'the,' 'a,' 'my,' or 'some').

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can use common connecting words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'or' in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words, like 'big' or 'happy,' in their sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future.
description_plain: Your child can use action words to show if something happened in the past, is happening now, or will happen in the future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'I,' 'my,' 'they,' or 'someone' correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use single and plural nouns with the right verbs, like 'The cat runs' and 'The cats run.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use regular nouns, names of people or places, and show when something belongs to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses basic grammar rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words for school subjects and everyday life, and can figure out what new words mean when they read or hear them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands when words have special meanings or are used in creative ways, and can notice how words are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what unfamiliar words mean by using clues in the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language differently depending on the situation, and can understand more when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters, punctuation, and correct spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember things they have learned or find information to answer a question.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, participate in shared research and writing projects.
description_plain: With help, your child can work with others on simple research and writing projects.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use computers or tablets to write and share their work, sometimes with classmates.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child can stay on topic, answer questions, and add more details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: This skill will be taught starting in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write a story about something that happened, put the events in order, use words like 'first' or 'then,' and finish the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, give some facts, and wrap up their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion, say what they think about a topic or book, give a reason, and end their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write often, sometimes for longer projects and sometimes for shorter ones, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or facts to help explain their ideas or answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books or online, check if it is true, and use it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects to answer questions and show what they have learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology, like computers or the Internet, to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can make their writing better by planning, editing, and trying new ways to say things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas in a way that fits the assignment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using details and putting events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, using clear facts and good organization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to share their opinion and support it with good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and understand informational texts appropriately complex for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read and understand nonfiction books that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find what is the same and what is different between two books about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons an author gives to support their ideas in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a book to talk about the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between information from pictures and information from words in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use things like headings, tables of contents, or bold words to find important information in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to figure out what words and phrases mean in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell you the main idea of a text and share important details from it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and understand prose and poetry of appropriate complexity for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read and understand stories and poems that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how characters in different stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a story to describe the characters, where it happens, or what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information, drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.
description_plain: Your child can explain the difference between storybooks and books that give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help you imagine with your senses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe the characters, where the story happens, and the main events using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including important details, and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand both stories and informational texts that are just right for their grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can talk about how two or more books cover similar topics or ideas and compare how the authors present them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the reasons and evidence an author gives to support their ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and use information from different sources, like pictures, charts, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell how the author’s point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a text, like sentences and paragraphs, fit together to make the whole story or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text and how the author’s word choices affect the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, or ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea or theme in a text and summarize the most important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says and use details from the text to explain their thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read first grade books out loud correctly, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read first grade books with understanding and for a reason.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read first grade words that are not spelled the usual way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have endings like -ed, -ing, or -s.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child can sound out two-syllable words by breaking them into parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and can count the syllables in a word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final –e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows how silent 'e' and vowel pairs make long vowel sounds in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read simple one-syllable words that follow regular spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read common letter pairs that make one sound, like 'ch' or 'sh.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use first grade phonics skills to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Substitute and delete parts of blends in the initial position in one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can change or remove the first sounds in simple words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add, substitute, and delete syllables in compound two-and three-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can add, change, or remove whole parts (syllables) in longer words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words, including words with initial and ending blends, into their complete sequence of individual phonemes.
description_plain: Your child can break apart spoken one-syllable words into all their individual sounds, even if the word has blends at the beginning or end.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final phonemes in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can pick out and say the first, middle, and last sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending phonemes, including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say a one-syllable word out loud by putting together individual sounds, including groups of consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in words with only one syllable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and phonemes.
description_plain: Your child understands how spoken words are made up of smaller parts like syllables and individual sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence.
description_plain: Your child can recognize what makes a sentence, such as starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized, like reading left to right and knowing what a word or sentence looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child will speak in complete sentences when needed to give clear answers or more information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child can make interesting audio recordings of stories or poems, reading smoothly and clearly, and can add pictures or visuals to help explain details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: With help, your child can talk about a topic, tell a story, or share an experience using clear facts and details, speaking so others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what a speaker says, giving good explanations and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the main ideas and important details from something read aloud or shown in different ways, like videos or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain their own ideas and what they understand during a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to make sure they understand, stay on topic, and connect their comments to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions.
description_plain: Your child can follow the agreed rules for group discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: With help, your child comes to discussions ready, having read or learned about the topic, and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, listen to others, build on their ideas, and share their own thoughts clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change the way they speak depending on the situation, and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and visuals, like charts or pictures, to help explain information and make presentations clearer.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that others can follow, organizing it to fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can judge what a speaker thinks, how they explain their ideas, and what evidence they use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and evaluate information from different sources, like videos, charts, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can get ready for and join in conversations with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words for conversations, school subjects, and special topics, including words that show where or when something happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that are similar but show different feelings or levels of certainty.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use.
description_plain: Your child can give real-life examples of how certain words are used.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context.
description_plain: Your child can tell when words or phrases mean exactly what they say or something different (like idioms) in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can notice small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries, beginning dictionaries, and other reference materials, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use glossaries, simple dictionaries, or other resources (in books or online) to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root.
description_plain: Your child can use a familiar root word to help figure out the meaning of a new word with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what a new word means when a familiar prefix or suffix is added to a word they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out what a word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.
description_plain: Your child can notice and follow the differences between how we speak and how we write in standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child can pick words and phrases that make their writing or speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other resources (in books or online) to check and correct their spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations in writing words.
description_plain: Your child can use common spelling patterns and rules to help spell words when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words and words they have learned, and can add endings to base words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.
description_plain: Your child can show when something belongs to someone by using possessives correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly when writing addresses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters for the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'and,' 'but,' 'because,' and 'although' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'bigger,' 'biggest,' 'more quickly,' and 'most quickly' correctly when comparing things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child can make sure the subject and verb in a sentence match, and that pronouns refer to the right nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs in the past, present, and future tense correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use both regular verbs (like 'walked') and irregular verbs (like 'ran') correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use abstract nouns, like 'freedom' or 'happiness,' in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make regular and irregular nouns plural, like 'cats' and 'children.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify, explain, and use nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs.
description_plain: Your child can identify and use nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses a variety of words for school subjects and can figure out new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figures of speech, how words are related, and the slight differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using clues in the sentence, word parts, or looking them up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use language in different ways depending on the situation and can understand more when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: With scaffolding, guidance, and support, write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: With help, your child will write regularly for both long and short assignments on different topics and for different audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: This skill starts in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from their own experiences or find information from books and websites, take short notes, and sort facts into groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, use technology to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child can use technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With help, your child can write in a way that fits the assignment and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can end their story in a way that wraps it up.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'first,' 'next,' and 'finally' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue and describe what characters do, think, and feel to show what happens in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can set up a story by introducing the situation and characters, and organize the events in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can end their informative writing with a closing statement or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'also,' 'another,' and 'for example' to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child can explain a topic by giving facts, definitions, and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful in aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic, group related information together, and use pictures to help explain when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or give information about a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can end their opinion writing with a closing statement or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'because,' 'so,' and 'for example' to connect their opinion and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic or book, state their opinion, and organize their reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write about their opinion on a topic or book and give reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly, sometimes working on longer projects over several days and sometimes finishing shorter pieces in one sitting, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or nonfiction books to support their ideas, opinions, or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can find useful information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it in their own words without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects by asking questions and showing what they have learned about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and the Internet to write, share their work, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, editing, making changes, or trying new ways to express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the assignment and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events, using good details and putting the events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain or teach about a topic, making sure the information is clear and organized.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write to share their opinion and support it with good reasons and evidence from what they have read or learned.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 2–3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles about history, science, and other topics on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find similarities and differences in the main points and important details of two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how certain sentences and paragraphs in a text are connected to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in a text to help them understand what they are reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between someone describing an event they experienced themselves and someone telling about it secondhand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use things like headings, tables of contents, or search tools to quickly find information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words and phrases in a text about a third-grade topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps in a process are related by time, order, or cause and effect in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, list the key details, and explain how those details support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what they read, using the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 2-3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the messages, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters.
description_plain: Your child can compare and contrast the messages, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about the same or similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: This standard does not apply to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the pictures in a story help show what is happening or how the characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own point of view and that of the narrator or characters in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about different parts of stories, plays, and poems, using words like chapter, scene, or stanza, and explain how each part builds on what came before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when the author uses nonliteral language.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can describe the characters in a story and explain how their actions affect what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount and summarize stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral, and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell and summarize stories from different cultures, find the main message or lesson, and explain how it is shown through important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what they read in stories, using the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand both stories and nonfiction texts on their own, even when the material is challenging.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about similar themes or topics to learn more or see how different authors approach the subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can identify and judge the arguments and claims in a text, deciding if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and evaluate information that is presented in different ways, such as in pictures, charts, or words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how the parts of a text, like sentences, paragraphs, or chapters, fit together and support the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including technical or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how and why people, events, or ideas change and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what the text says, make logical guesses, and use specific examples from the text to support their answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or double-check the meaning and pronunciation of words, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with expression, getting better each time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level books with understanding and a clear reason for reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multisyllable words.
description_plain: Your child can sound out and read longer words with more than one syllable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common suffixes. Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can read words with common endings and can also read tricky words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can recognize common beginnings and endings of words and knows what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use phonics and word skills to figure out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 3 standard for this concept.
description_plain: There is no standard for this concept in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: There is not a grade 3 standard for this concept.
description_plain: There is no standard for this concept in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English and situations where informal discourse is appropriate; use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child understands when to use formal language and when it’s okay to speak more casually, and can use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add sound recordings or pictures to their presentations to help explain their main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can clearly and in order talk about a topic, story, or experience, using facts and details to support their main points and speaking at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can pick out the reasons and evidence a speaker gives to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate parts of something they heard or saw in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can review the main ideas from a discussion and explain their own thoughts based on what was talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions to better understand the topic, and add comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow the group’s rules during discussions and do their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready, having read or learned about the topic, and uses what they know to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different types of group discussions, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and knows when to use formal English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to help explain information in presentations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can present information and evidence in a way that is organized and easy for listeners to follow, using the right style for the situation.

subject: Language Arts
